name: Eiffel Tower
description: No other monument better symbolizes Paris.

name: Arc de Triomphe de l'Étoile
description: The Arc de Triomphe exudes grandeur and offers a central view of the city.

name: Champs-Élysées
description: Widely regarded to be one of the most recognizable avenues in the world

name: Notre Dame Cathedral
description: ImpressiveGothiccathedral that was the inspiration for Victor Hugo's novelThe Hunchback of Notre Dame.

name: Sacré Coeur
description: A church perched on top of the highest point in Paris. Behind the church is the artists' area, in front are spectacular views of the whole city.

name: Opera Garnier
description: Masterpiece of theatre architecture of the 19th century built by Charles Garnier and inaugurated in 1875 housing the Paris Opera since it was founded by Louis XIV.

name: Catacombes de Paris
description: Used to store the exhumed bones from the overflowing Paris cemetery. The bones of more than 6 million bodies lie here.

name: Grande Arche de la Défense
description: A modern office-building variant of the Arc de Triomphe.

name: Panthéon
description: Underneath, the final resting place for the great heroes of the French Republic including Voltaire, Victor Hugo, and Marie Skłodowska-Curie; above, a marvellous view of the city.

name: Père-Lachaise Cemetery
description: Unlike any cemetery in the world. Ornate grave stones, monuments set among tree lined lanes. See the graves of Jim Morrison, Oscar Wilde, and Frédéric Chopin, among many others.

name: Sainte-Chapelle
description: Exquisite stained glass chapel. More beautiful interior than the gloomy Notre Dame Cathedral.

name: The Louvre
description: One of the finest museums in the world of art and culture. Home of the Mona Lisa and innumerable others. Enormous building and collection, plan at least two visits..

name: Musée d'Orsay
description: Incredible collection housed in a former railway station. Works by the great artists of the 19th century (1848-1914) including Monet's "Blue Water Lilies, Renoir's "Bal du moulin de la Galette", van Gogh's "Bedroom in Arles", Whistler's "The Artists Mother", etc.

name: Rodin Museum
description: Collection and archives dedicated to the sculptor Auguste Rodin, in a charming home with garden.

name: Picasso Museum
description: Contains the master's own collection.

name: Musée Marmottan-Monet
description: Over 300 paintings of Claude Monet. Also, the works of Berthe Morisot, Edgar Degas, Édouard Manet and Pierre-Auguste Renoir. "Impression Soleil Levant" by Monet is on display.

name: Musée de l'Orangerie
description: [Jardin des Tuileries] Houses "The Water Lilies" (or "Nymphéas") - a 360 degree depiction of Monet's flower garden at Giverny. Also, impressionist and post-Impressionist paintings by Cézanne, Matisse, Modigliani, Picasso, Renoir, Rousseau, Soutine, Sisley and others.

name: Musée Delacroix
description: Housed in the home of painter Eugène Delacroix.

name: Centre Georges Pompidou
description: The museum of modern art. The building and adjoining Stravinsky Fountain are attractions in themselves. Closed for renovation until 2030.

name: Les Invalides
description: Very impressive museum of arms and armor from the Middle Ages to today. Also contains the tomb of Napoleon Bonaparte.

name: Musée national du Moyen Âge
description: A medieval museum exhibiting the five "The Lady and the Unicorn" tapestries, housed in a part Roman, part medieval building.

name: Musée des Arts Décoratifs
description: Showcasing eight centuries of French savoir-faire.

name: Carnavalet
description: Museum of Paris history; exhibitions are permanent and free. Renovated 2017–2021.

name: Cité des sciences et de l’industrie - La Villette
description: Science museum primarily for children.

name: Mémorial de la Shoah
description: Paris's Holocaust Memorial Museum, in the heart of the Marais on rue Geoffroy l'Asnier. Free Entry, weekly guided tours. Second Sunday of the month there is a free tour in English.

name: Jacquemart-Andre Museum
description: Private collection of French, Italian, and Dutch masterpieces in a typical 19th-century mansion.

name: Guimet Museum
description: One of the largest collections outside Asia of Southeast Asian, Indian, Afghan, Chinese, Japanese, and Korean art.

name: French Open
description: One of the fourtennisGrand Slam tournaments. It is scheduled for two weeks from the last Sunday in May. The event, on clay courts, features the world’s top-ranked tennis players.

name: Stade de France
description: France's national stadium has a capacity of 81,338, and hosts concerts and various events. Soccer clubs sometimes play here when their home stadium is unavailable.

name: Maison Européene de la Photographie
description: Important center for contemporary photography with a large exposition area having three or four major expositions per year dedicated to contemporary photography. Housed in an updatedhôtel particulierbuilt in 1706. There is a large library, auditorium and café. Their archive houses over twenty thousand works of art. Admission is free every Wednesday from 17:00 to 20:00.

name: Better Paris Photos
description: Better Paris Photos offers instructional tours and workshops that combine hands-on learning of essential photographic techniques with guiding to, and commentary about, the most photogenic spots of Paris. Led by English-speaking photographers and instructors, these tours are open to all skill levels and interest.
